title: Claim-ready owners and reusable JobCore driver adoption
summary: Add exact claim facts, owner-versioned prepared mutation surfaces, reusable JobCore token custody, and generation-aware adoption rollback and terminal closure.
acceptance: Food, Rest, Medical, Hauling and Lamp expose exact allocation-free claim-fact reads carrying every reviewed subject, target, stack, fixture, caregiver, cell, quantity, owner, row, index, backlog, schedule, weather, ability, version and generation scalar without reinterpreting an owner scalar as EntityId; B3 ordinary WorkOffer mapping may produce only Hauling claims

ItemStack, storage logistics, Need, Health and medical owners expose the minimum reviewed version-bound caller-owned mutation prepare/commit surfaces required by the historical drivers; every prepare validates all exact identities, quantities, versions and version headroom before any mutation, and no foreign owner identity is fabricated or inferred

M4 exposes exact Lamp/ItemStack reads plus caller-owned prepares and package-internal non-failing commits; the prepared payload contains no foreign owner identity, dirty-queue/createSnapshot/version/hash coverage expands for the changed Lamp owner, and this task does not add an M4 restore API

JobCore owns an O(1) exact owner index sized by ownerCapacity across legacy and autonomous jobs, tracks occupied/empty slots, legacyLiveCount, owner-affine reusable job tokens, generations and slot versions, clears every terminal path, rejects cross-generation rebind and stale owners, and preserves the complete index/token lifecycle through snapshot, hash, restore and focused tests

Eating, Rest, Hauling and Treatment drivers support combined claim-time adoption using committed owner reads, exact claim id/allocationEpoch bindings and positive JobCore generation; guarded rollback restores every reversible owner mutation, advances the pending and basis slot versions consistently, and uses Float64-safe Ticks including overflow-safe Rest duration multiplication

All reviewed driver effects and cleanup phases are explicit and once-only across success, permanent failure and retryable failure; domain effects complete before exact claim release, and after claim release only non-failing driver, JobCore and autonomy bookkeeping tails remain

Snapshots, hashes, restore validation and package exports cover every changed owner and JobCore field with deterministic stable iteration; M4 follows its reviewed createSnapshot/hash-only stance and no compatibility shape is silently synthesized

A receiver-exact TypeScript TypeChecker closure roots every new claim-fact, owner prepare/commit and JobCore adoption/recovery surface, proves caller-owned output identity and bounded loops, and rejects allocation, materializing compatibility reads, unresolved receivers, broad cleanup and foreign-owner access in every reachable body

Focused regressions cover stale owner bases, allocationEpoch ABA, occupied and empty JobCore recovery, four version-boundary transitions, combined adoption rollback, once-only effects and cleanup, then seeds 1 through 4 run the four historical scenarios for 100000 ticks each without invariant, hash or determinism regressions

Legacy WorkOffer register, update and remove roots share the Into preflight, validate global and row version headroom before any bucket, payload, active-count, owner-version or version write, allow FFFE to advance to FFFF, and reject any exhausted lane all-before-any with the exact work_offer_index_version_exhausted reason

When Storage refresh encounters WorkOffer version exhaustion, WorkOffer remains byte-for-byte unchanged while Storage completes its reviewed derived, queue and version update with mirror-tolerant offerActive semantics; this task adds no WorkOffer snapshot, hash or schema, Storage return protocol, or cross-owner rollback

This WorkOffer scope amendment is a narrow exception to the approved plan prohibition on generic WorkOffer workarounds: it repairs only the legacy no-wrap safety gap directly exercised by Storage and does not remap candidates or claim facts onto generic WorkOffers or expand BuildSite, M3, benchmark or schema scope

The deterministic-quality compatibility amendment is limited to monotonic M3 ordinary-life timing and focused replay goldens, one BuildSite reverse-Tick rollback fixture, duplicate Lamp command rejection in the playable command slice, and two reviewed sim-worker test hash literals; it adds no BuildSite feature, partial Need commit, JobCore terminal occupancy rollback, sim-worker production change, schema, benchmark, baseline, admission or release scope

The task changes only the frozen claim-ready owners, JobCore, four historical drivers, M4 fact/mutation surfaces, focused tests and its own coordination artifacts; it does not edit ReservationLedger custody, ResidentAutonomyStore/B3 coordinator files, integrated GameSession, protocol/product files, dependencies, benchmarks, baselines, admission or release surfaces

history.details: Created as the second of exactly two serial prerequisites after independent B3 PLAN PASS at 7a483fc54701eeddb0e3285929dadb2971880075; remains proposed until WM-0182 is independently verified, integrated and done.

WORK_OFFER_REPAIR_SLICE_FREEZE_PASS: narrow exception to the approved plan prohibition on generic WorkOffer workarounds, limited to the legacy no-wrap gap directly exercised by Storage; added WorkOffer production, focused tests and owner hot-path closure evidence without candidate or claim-fact remapping or BuildSite, M3, benchmark or schema expansion; state and simulation-engineer claim remain unchanged.

Approved narrow deterministic-quality compatibility repair for monotonic M3 ordinary-life timing and focused replay goldens, a BuildSite reverse-Tick rollback fixture, duplicate Lamp command rejection in the playable command slice, and reviewed sim-worker test hash literals; no BuildSite feature expansion, partial Need commits, JobCore terminal occupancy rollback, sim-worker production change, schema, benchmark, baseline, admission or release scope; state and simulation-engineer claim remain unchanged.

Implemented claim-ready owner seams, reusable JobCore custody, four driver adoption/cleanup, Lamp snapshot/hash closure, no-wrap owner boundaries, and deterministic quality compatibility repairs; required focused groups, full quality, control gates, and four 100000-tick scenarios passed. See coordination/reports/WM-0183.md.
verified: No blocking findings; acceptance evidence confirmed

Fast-forwarded commits 49e123e, 140bc57 and 162aae0 onto main; exact-main quality passed 83 files/1088 tests in 223.81s, four 100000-tick scenarios matched expected hashes, and taskctl/handoff/boundaries/format/diff gates passed.

singleWriterRule: WM-0183 begins only after WM-0182 is independently verified, integrated and done. Until then WM-0182 is the sole ready repair writer and WM-0170 remains blocked with its approved B2 checkpoint and original owner.
integrationOrder: Second prerequisite. Promote and claim only after WM-0182 is done; independent reviewer verification, project-director integration and done are mandatory before WM-0170 may resume.
